summary: Germany, France and the United Kingdom are discussing possible negotiations with Russia to end the war. Ukrainian intelligence reports Russia can launch up to 100 ballistic missiles per month and plans to deliver up to 60 Kinzhal hypersonic missiles this year. Multiple UAV attacks have been reported across northern and southern Ukrainian regions, targeting logistics centres and infrastructure.
key_points: Germany, France and the United Kingdom are discussing the possibility of beginning negotiations with Russia to end the war (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30665).

Russian intelligence claims Russia can employ up to 100 ballistic missiles per month and is producing 55‑60 missiles monthly, with plans to deliver up to 60 Kinzhal hypersonic missiles this year (Source: @UkraineNow/81671).

Ukrainian forces report several UAV attacks: drones from Chernihiv heading to Pryluky, from Kharkiv northward, and from the Black Sea toward Odesa and Chornomorsk (Source: @kpszsu/63958, @kpszsu/64001).
A high threat alert was issued for possible Iskander‑M ballistic missile launches from the Bryansk region (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67473).
Ukraine is planning to create recruiting centres abroad to attract foreign volunteers, offering salaries up to $10,000 per month for dangerous missions (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30685).
Ukrainian MP Roman Kaptelov announced the recruitment centre plan, while NATO Secretary General Rutte pledged that NATO will not let Russia win (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30669).
Russian President Putin stated that a cease‑fire is not required for negotiations and emphasized controlling the Donbas (Source: @tass_agency/379182).

sections.executive_summary: Germany, France and the United Kingdom are discussing possible negotiations with Russia to end the war. Russian missile production capacity remains high, with up to 100 ballistic missiles possible per month and planned delivery of 60 Kinzhal hypersonic missiles. UAV attacks continue across multiple fronts, targeting logistics hubs and civilian infrastructure.

points: Ukraine plans to establish recruiting centres abroad to attract foreign volunteers, offering salaries up to $10,000 per month for the most dangerous combat missions, as stated by MP Roman Kaptelov (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30685).
Ukrainian forces reported a UAV attack on a logistics centre of Nova Poshta in Dnipro, with damage assessment ongoing (Source: @UkraineNow/81676).
A UAV strike hit an industrial facility in the Boryspil district of Kyiv region, injuring a worker who received medical care (Source: @UkraineNow/81655).
Ukrainian MP Roman Kaptelov also highlighted the recruitment initiative, linking it to higher pay for high‑risk missions (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30685).
Ukrainian authorities reported a UAV group from the Black Sea heading toward Odesa, targeting Odesa and Chornomorsk (Source: @kpszsu/64001).
Russian intelligence claims the ability to launch up to 100 ballistic missiles per month and maintains a steady production rate of 55‑60 missiles monthly (Source: @UkraineNow/81671).
Russia plans to deliver up to 60 Kinzhal hypersonic aerobalistic missiles this year (Source: @UkraineNow/81671).
A high threat alert was issued for possible Iskander‑M ballistic missile launches from the Bryansk region (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67473).
President Putin stated that a cease‑fire is not necessary for negotiations and emphasized Russia's control over the Donbas region (Source: @tass_agency/379182).
Putin indicated Russia could supply 25‑28 billion cubic meters of gas per year via the remaining Nord Stream pipeline segment, pending German government approval (Source: @tass_agency/379182).
UAVs from Chernihiv were observed heading toward Pryluky in the north‑eastern sector (Source: @kpszsu/63958).
A reactive UAV from Chernihiv was reported moving toward Poltava, targeting the Mirhorod direction (Source: @kpszsu/63991).
UAV activity was noted over Sumy region, with a group passing near Yampil moving southwest (Source: @kpszsu/64012).
Ukrainian forces reported a UAV group from Kursk region (Russian side) heading toward Konotop in Sumy (Source: @kpszsu/64001).
A UAV from Kharkiv was seen moving northward, indicating activity in the eastern operational area (Source: @kpszsu/63991).
UAVs were reported over Kharkiv heading north, suggesting reconnaissance or strike missions (Source: @kpszsu/63991).
A UAV from Chernihiv was observed heading toward Nizhyn from the southeast (Source: @kpszsu/63981).
UAV activity over Zhytomyr region moving past Ovruch from the northeast was reported (Source: @kpszsu/63978).
A UAV group from the Black Sea region was directed toward Odesa and Chornomorsk, indicating southern‑northern axis operations (Source: @kpszsu/64001).
UAVs were observed over Zaporizhzhia (Hortitsia, Pershotravneve) indicating continued southern‑northern pressure (Source: @eRadarrua/95384).
Russian drones attacked infrastructure in the Kyiv region, striking an industrial object in the Boryspil district (Source: @UkraineNow/81655).
A UAV strike hit a residential area in Zaporizhzhia, causing damage to a multi‑storey building and ten injuries (Source: @UkraineNow/81681).
Ukrainian officials reported a UAV attack on the Zaporizhzhia thermal power station, noting smoke and sounds of military activity (Source: @UkraineNow/81666).
A UAV group from the Black Sea was directed toward Odesa and Chornomorsk, targeting southern ports (Source: @kpszsu/64001).
Russian forces reportedly lost approximately 400 transport vehicles in a day due to medium‑range drone strikes, according to a post (Source: @eRadarrua/95388).
High threat alert for possible Iskander‑M ballistic missile launches from Bryansk region (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67473).
Ukrainian reports of multiple UAV incursions across various fronts, including Chernihiv, Kharkiv, Sumy, and the Black Sea (Sources: @kpszsu/63958, @kpszsu/64001).
Russian President Putin stated that a cease‑fire is not required for negotiations, implying continued air operations (Source: @tass_agency/379182).
Ukrainian forces reported a UAV attack on a logistics centre in Dnipro, indicating continued air‑borne logistical targeting (Source: @UkraineNow/81676).
Russian missile production capacity remains high, with up to 100 ballistic missiles possible per month (Source: @UkraineNow/81671).

key_developments: Germany, France and the United Kingdom are discussing the possibility of beginning negotiations with Russia to end the war (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30665).

Russian intelligence claims the capability to launch up to 100 ballistic missiles per month and is producing 55‑60 missiles monthly, with plans to deliver up to 60 Kinzhal hypersonic missiles this year (Source: @UkraineNow/81671).
Ukrainian forces reported multiple UAV attacks across the front, including drones from Chernihiv heading to Pryluky and from the Black Sea toward Odesa (Source: @kpszsu/63958, @kpszsu/64001).
A high threat alert was issued for possible Iskander‑M ballistic missile launches from the Bryansk region (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67473).
Ukraine plans to create recruiting centres abroad to attract foreign volunteers, offering salaries up to $10,000 per month for dangerous missions (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30685).
President Putin stated that a cease‑fire is not required for negotiations and emphasized Russia's control over the Donbas (Source: @tass_agency/379182).
Ukrainian MP Roman Kaptelov announced the recruitment centre plan, linking it to higher pay for high‑risk missions (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30685).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian missile production remains robust, with the capacity to launch up to 100 ballistic missiles monthly and the planned delivery of 60 Kinzhal hypersonic missiles, posing a significant long‑range threat. UAV activity across multiple fronts indicates continued Russian aerial pressure on logistics and civilian infrastructure. The high‑threat alert for Iskander‑M launches from Bryansk adds a tactical ballistic risk to northern Ukrainian regions.
sections.regional_response: Western allies, notably Germany, France and the United Kingdom, are exploring negotiation pathways, suggesting a diplomatic shift. NATO reaffirmed its commitment to prevent Russian victory, while Ukraine seeks to bolster its forces through foreign recruitment centres offering high salaries. Regional actors remain focused on mitigating UAV and missile threats while maintaining defensive postures.
sections.intelligence_notes: Ukrainian intelligence reports precise UAV trajectories, enabling targeted air‑defence allocations on the eastern and southern fronts. Russian statements downplaying the need for a cease‑fire indicate a willingness to continue offensive operations despite diplomatic overtures. Monitoring of missile production rates and Kinzhal deliveries is critical for forecasting future strike capabilities.
